textPlain: I've given up on custom launchers altogether because their proper support for gesture navigation breaks regularly. Nova support blamed this on missing apis, caused by manufacturer error (or intention?). Either way, custom launchers don't seem compatible with mainstream usage anymore.

I don't fully understand the situation. This statement [1] seems to indicate that Nova doesn't have to open-source it:> Cliff: Branch owns Nova completely and Kevin has no access to it in any way at all. So no, he can't re-brand, keep coding and go forward. That's a breach of contract on his behalf....but this post says they are obligated to:> Cliff: Much more to it than word of mouth. There's a contract as well.[1] https://infosec.exchange/@cliffwade/115168015274422682
Guess it's time to find a new launcher then. Suggestions?

A year ago there were some posts about Nova being on shaky ground because Barry was the only one left working on it. This seems to just be continuing the negative narrative. Unfortunate. Seems like he was really close to open sourcing it, which would probably be a boon. It's a longstanding and still pretty popular launcher in the Android world isn't it? I've used it for years on multiple phones.
I signed the linked petition at https://www.change.org/p/make-nova-launcher-open-source-let-...

I've been using lawnchair, and I like it's simplicity. They're latest "real" released version is from 2018, but not long ago they jumped from Version 14-beta3 to 15-beta1. I can't say I understand their development philosophy, but the product is pretty decent with an occasional issue. I have had some intermittent problems with widgets on this latest beta.https://github.com/LawnchairLauncher/lawnchair

I looked at several yesterday.  I landed on Action Launcher because it has a scrollable dock and infinite scrolling homepages.  It lacks tabs in the app drawer, but it does let you create folders there.Hyperion seemed to have all necessary features, but the UI was unintuitive and the documentation non-existent.I also liked Lynx, but that would be a major change for me.
Long time KISS user. Highly recommend, as it makes my app use far more intentional.
I like Niagra.I'm bummed Sesame got bought by these guys and stopped updating for no ultimate reason.
